candidate_problems.text: What are the criticism and test criteria that distinguish genuinely knowledge-creating analogies from merely suggestive or decorative similarities? The conjecture says analogy yields new knowledge when it identifies a load-bearing abstract structure, but it leaves open how, in practice, to detect that the mapped relation is genuinely indispensable rather than retrospectively redescribed after success.

How can a newly posited higher-order structure be individuated and evaluated as a real explanatory object rather than a convenient abstraction? The conjecture treats the abstract relation uncovered by analogy as the true product of reasoning, but it remains unresolved what makes such structures objective, non-arbitrary, and capable of supporting progress across domains.

What is the boundary between analogical reasoning and general theory formation or concept creation? If analogy works by conjecturing a deeper shared structure that reorganizes both domains, then it becomes unclear what is distinctive about analogy itself, when the source-target framing ceases to matter, and whether all major explanatory advances should be redescribed as analogical in this stronger sense.
